_comment: M9 Nim NATIVE-path calltrace — an ILLUSTRATIVE committed example.
The REAL calltrace the test drives is regenerated at test time into a
temp dir pointing at the freshly-built binary, with the 'binary' field set
to that binary and each 'functionName' set to the MANGLED symbol name
discovered by running `nm` on THAT binary. Nim mangles proc names via the C
backend: with `-g` (this fixture's build.sh) the dev-shell Nim emits the
Itanium-style `_ZN<len>calc<len>usedAE` shown below; without `-g` it emits
Either way the names MUST come from the binary's own symbol table — see
The executed set is the two pure leaves {usedA, usedB}; mainCalc (the
call-containing, relocation-sensitive proc) is deliberately excluded, and
unusedC is emitted-but-not-executed, so neither appears here.
binary: <filled in at test time: path to the nim-compiled calc binary>
